message:
fix(widgets): extend NoStyling/Skeleton degradation to block and panel widgets

Seventh wave of the degradation-handling cleanup.  Block and
Panel both stored their border/title styles internally and
applied them unconditionally at render time, bypassing the
DegradationLevel check.  Same fix pattern as the earlier waves:
thread a runtime-resolved style through the internal helpers.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/block.rs

- Block::border_cell(c) refactored to border_cell(c, style) —
  accept the runtime-resolved style as an explicit argument
  instead of reading self.border_style.
- Block::render_borders(area, buf) refactored to
  render_borders(area, buf, style) — threaded through to every
  border_cell call in the corner + edge rendering loops
  (LEFT/RIGHT/TOP/BOTTOM edges and the four corner cells).
- Block::render() (the public entry point) now snapshots deg
  from frame.buffer, short-circuits on !deg.render_content(),
  and computes the effective border style:
    let border_style = if deg.apply_styling() {
        self.border_style
    } else {
        Style::default()
    };
  then passes it through to render_borders().
- Title style gets the same treatment: `if deg.apply_styling()`
  picks self.title_style, otherwise Style::default(), before
  the title draw_text_span call.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/panel.rs

- Panel's render() does the same deg snapshot +
  render_content() short-circuit + conditional style selection
  for its background fill, border, and title decorations.
  Panel delegates its border drawing to Block internally, so
  the Panel layer only has to compute the effective styles
  and hand them to Block::render via the existing
  Panel::to_block() pathway.
- A new test render_no_styling_drops_border_and_title_colors
  asserts that under NoStyling a configured panel with
  distinctive border + title colors produces the same buffer
  contents as a bare panel.
- A new test render_skeleton_is_noop asserts that under
  Skeleton the panel's render() is a no-op against a pristine
  sentinel buffer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
